topic.what: Pre-commit execution scheme to mask intermittent ordering failures in BFT SMR
topic.how: Uses adaptive probabilistic speculation on DAG-based consensus to speculatively execute transactions ahead of confirmed ordering, with commit-aware snapshots for fast recovery
topic.why_matters: Existing BFT SMR systems suffer high transaction latency when ordering stalls; this work enables near-instantaneous commitment after ordering recovers by pre-executing during stalls

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time for transactions to be confirmed
characteristics.context: p99 latency under normal execution achieves 1.58x speedup; under ordering failures achieves 1.36x speedup on p99 recovery latency

characteristics.what_itasures: Time to drain accumulated backlog after ordering failure
characteristics.vs_baseline: Existing SMR systems require ~6 seconds catch-up phase
characteristics.context: Faster recovery from ordering failures via pre-commit execution results already available

characteristics.what_it_measures: Probability speculative order matches final consensus order
characteristics.evidence: APS leverages DAG structural information and voting patterns
characteristics.context: APS adaptively selects order most likely consistent with final consensus

characteristics.evidence: Only take snapshots when mis-speculation is suspected via reputation-based decision
characteristics.context: Commit-aware snapshot avoids excessive storage overhead

good_for: Reducing transaction latency under intermittent ordering failures
High-throughput blockchain systems with decoupled consensus
Systems with frequent network instability or leader failures
not_for: Fully synchronous networks where ordering rarely fails
Systems with extremely limited storage resources
Low-latency applications requiring deterministic ordering guarantees
requires: 3f+1 replica configuration with up to f Byzantine

novelty: First BFT SMR system designed to effectively mask performance degradation under intermittent ordering failures
Adaptive probabilistic speculation mechanism predicting consensus order from DAG structural information
Commit-aware snapshot mechanism minimizing re-execution overhead via reputation-based snapshot decisions
Integration of pre-commit execution with DAG-based consensus for speculative transaction processing
open_problems: Scaling APS mechanism for larger number of replicas
Handling sustained asynchrony periods (Sui incident lasted ~1 day)
Minimizing storage overhead for commit-aware snapshots in long-running systems
Extending to multi-chain BFT protocols beyond DAG-based approaches
